title: brachyscome aculeata
intro: Brachyscome aculeata, also known as prickly flax, is a perennial herb native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and diuretic properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Brachyscome aculeata are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
